_: Selector vocabulary for the QA harness. Drop a copy named qa.config.json next to your guide.html to override. Every rule below degrades to a no-op when its selectors match nothing, so an unfilled section is safe - but it is also unchecked. Fill in what your guide actually uses.
_geometry: Elements whose box is set by paper geometry (mm) or margin:auto pins. Their spacing is not a scale violation.
_chrome: Whole regions exempt from the spacing scale (a cover is a composition, not a rhythm).
_scale: The spacing scale, in px. 2 is a documented half-step for tabular density. Anything off this list is an ad-hoc value, which is where layout rot starts.
_components: Self-contained islands. A page-level rule must never reach inside one of these. Used by scope-lint and by the leak detector.
_marginColumn: Rows with a display glyph hanging in a margin column beside a text block. Checks ink-top cap alignment and a constant optical gutter. glyph/text are resolved within each row.
_capAlign: Extra ink-top pairs that are not margin-column rows (e.g. a band's display letter vs its eyebrow).
_columnAlign: Does the grid actually hold across components? Each entry asserts two elements share a left edge.

columnAlign.label: band letter on the numeral column
band eyebrow on the title column

_bandGap: Gap between a full-bleed band and the first content block beneath it.
_rhythm: Repeated blocks that must share a top padding across the whole document.
_pills: Must never wrap to a second line.
_wrapRows: Flex/grid rows where a lone item on the last line reads as a mistake.
_uniformRows: Repeated siblings that must all be the same height (an unequal one is a wrapping row).
_prose: Blocks checked for one-word last lines (widows).
_crops: Components screenshotted individually at high zoom. THESE ARE THE IMAGES YOU MUST OPEN. A full-page thumbnail cannot show you a 161px void or an orphaned chip.
_motion: Screen-only micro-interaction contract. entranceClass is added when a slice scrolls into view; ambientClass while it stays there.
